seo.title: Invoicemonk vs Wave (2026): Free Invoicing With Audit Trail vs US/Canada Only
seo.description: Invoicemonk vs Wave in 2026: both offer free invoicing, but Invoicemonk works globally with multi-currency, credit notes and an immutable audit trail. Wave is US/Canada only and limited to USD/CAD.

hero.title: Invoicemonk vs Wave (2026)
hero.subtitle: Both have free plans. Invoicemonk works globally with multi-currency, credit notes and an immutable audit trail on every plan. Wave is restricted to the US and Canada and invoices only in USD or CAD.

overview: Short answer: Invoicemonk is the better choice if you live anywhere outside the US or Canada, invoice in any non-USD/CAD currency, or need an immutable audit trail and credit notes. Wave is the better choice only if you are a US or Canada-based solo freelancer, invoice exclusively in USD or CAD, and need free US payroll add-ons.

Geography is the deciding factor. Wave has restricted new signups to the US and Canada since 2023 and supports invoicing only in USD or CAD. Invoicemonk works in every country with internet access and includes multi-currency on every plan — Starter (free, 5 invoices/mo), Pro ($29/mo, unlimited), SME ($129/mo, unlimited users) and Biz (custom, e-invoicing & government submission).

Compliance is the second gap. Every Invoicemonk invoice is hashed into an immutable audit trail and carries a QR code anyone can scan to verify authenticity — features that ship on the free Starter plan. Wave does not offer credit notes (you have to issue a negative invoice as a workaround), an immutable audit log, or invoice verification.

keyDifferences.title: What are the key differences between Invoicemonk and Wave?

items.title: Where can each be used?
items.desc: Wave restricted new signups to the US and Canada in 2023. Invoicemonk works worldwide with country-specific tax presets for Nigeria (FIRS), UK (HMRC MTD), US (IRS), Canada (CRA), Australia (ATO), Saudi Arabia (ZATCA) and EU PEPPOL.

items.title: How many currencies are supported?
items.desc: Wave only invoices in USD and CAD. Invoicemonk supports every major currency (USD, EUR, GBP, NGN, INR, CAD, AUD, ZAR, KES and more) on every plan, including the free Starter tier.

items.title: Can each handle credit notes and corrections?
items.desc: Wave has no native credit-note support — you have to issue a negative invoice. Invoicemonk has built-in credit notes that link to the original invoice, preserve the audit chain and keep books reconciled.

items.title: What audit-trail protection do you get?
items.desc: Wave offers basic activity logs. Invoicemonk hashes every invoice, payment, edit and credit note into an append-only audit trail on every plan — your year-end and tax-audit defense without an upgrade.

invoicemonk.title: Choose Invoicemonk if you…
items: Are based outside the US or Canada
Invoice (or might invoice) in EUR, GBP, NGN, INR, AUD or any non-USD/CAD currency
Need credit notes, refund tracking or invoice corrections
Want an immutable audit trail and QR-verified invoices
Need team access as your business grows

competitor.title: Choose Wave if you…
items: Are a US- or Canada-based solo freelancer
Only invoice in USD or CAD
Want a free US payroll add-on inside the same tool
Don't need credit notes, multi-currency or team access
Are happy without an immutable audit trail

migration.title: How do I migrate from Wave to Invoicemonk?

steps.title: Export your Wave data
steps.desc: Wave → Settings → Data Export. Download customers, invoices and transactions as CSV.

steps.title: Create your Invoicemonk account
steps.desc: Sign up free at app.invoicemonk.com. Add business profile, logo and tax ID in under 5 minutes.

steps.title: Import customers and history
steps.desc: Upload the Wave CSVs. Names, emails, addresses and invoice history map automatically.

steps.title: Set up multi-currency accounts
steps.desc: Add a currency account for every market you invoice — EUR, GBP, NGN and more — included on every plan.

steps.title: Send a globally-compliant invoice
steps.desc: Your first Invoicemonk invoice is hashed, QR-verifiable, and ready for any tax authority.

items.question: Is Invoicemonk better than Wave?
items.answer: For 95% of users — yes. Wave is restricted to the US and Canada and only invoices in USD or CAD. Invoicemonk works globally with multi-currency, credit notes, immutable audit trails and QR-verified invoices on every plan, including the free Starter tier (5 invoices/month). Wave still wins for US-only solo freelancers who need free integrated payroll.

items.question: Is Wave really free?
items.answer: Wave's invoicing and accounting are free, but the company monetizes through payment processing (2.9% + $0.60 per card transaction) and payroll. Invoicemonk's Starter plan is also free (5 invoices/mo) with no markup on payment processing — you pay your gateway (Stripe/Paystack/Flutterwave) directly.

items.question: Why doesn't Wave work in my country?
items.answer: Wave withdrew from all markets outside the US and Canada in 2023. If you signed up before that, your account may still work, but you can no longer add new features in restricted markets. Invoicemonk is built for global use with localized tax compliance for 8+ jurisdictions.

items.question: Does Wave support multi-currency?
items.answer: No. Wave only invoices in USD and CAD. If you have even one international client, Invoicemonk is the safer choice — multi-currency is included on the free Starter plan and every paid tier.

items.question: Can Wave issue credit notes?
items.answer: No. Wave has no native credit-note feature — refunds and corrections require manual workarounds (negative invoices) that break the audit trail. Invoicemonk has built-in credit notes that link to the original invoice and preserve the hash chain.

items.question: Which has stronger audit and compliance features?
items.answer: Invoicemonk by a wide margin. Every invoice, payment, edit and credit note is hashed into an immutable audit log on every plan. Each invoice carries a QR code clients and tax authorities can scan to verify. Wave offers neither.

items.question: Can I switch from Wave to Invoicemonk?
items.answer: Yes. Export customers, invoices and transactions from Wave as CSV, then bulk-import via Invoicemonk's dashboard. Most migrations take 15–30 minutes.

cta.title: Try the global, audit-ready alternative to Wave
cta.description: Free invoicing that works in every country — with multi-currency and immutable audit trail on every plan.
